name: Maryland SNAP Benefits
description: Maryland's SNAP program provides monthly food benefits on an EBT card through the Department of Human Services. Benefits help low-income individuals and families purchase groceries at authorized retailers.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 200% FPL for most households

name: Maryland Medicaid
description: Maryland's Medicaid program providing comprehensive health coverage to eligible low-income residents. Covers doctor visits, hospital care, prescriptions, behavioral health, dental, vision, and long-term care services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant individuals, seniors, and people with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults up to 138% FPL; children up to 317% FPL; pregnant individuals up to 259% FPL

name: Maryland Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial assistance to workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Benefits are calculated based on previous wages and can last up to 26 weeks.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own with sufficient earnings history
incomeLimitNotes: Weekly benefit based on previous wages; maximum weekly benefit applies

name: Maryland Department of Housing and Community Development
description: Provides affordable housing programs including rental assistance, homebuyer assistance, homelessness prevention, and community development. Administers multiple state and federal housing programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income homebuyers and renters in Maryland
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally targets households below 80% of area median income

name: Maryland Legal Aid
description: Maryland's largest provider of free civil legal services to low-income residents. Handles cases involving housing, family law, public benefits, consumer protection, employment, and immigration.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income Maryland residents with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of the federal poverty level

name: Maryland American Job Centers
description: Statewide network of workforce development centers providing free employment services including job search, career counseling, resume assistance, skills training, and employer connections.
eligibilitySummary: All Maryland job seekers and employers

name: Maryland LIHEAP - Energy Assistance (MEAP)
description: The Maryland Energy Assistance Program helps low-income households pay heating and electric bills. Provides one-time grants paid directly to utility companies on behalf of eligible households.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households at or below 175% of the federal poverty level
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 175% FPL

name: Maryland 211
description: Free, confidential service connecting Marylanders to local resources for food, housing, health care, utilities, and other essential services. Available 24/7 by phone and online.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Maryland seeking information about community resources
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services in 150+ languages

name: Maryland Food Bank
description: A Feeding America member food bank serving all of Maryland. Distributes food through a statewide network of over 1,300 partner organizations including food pantries, shelters, and meal programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in Maryland

name: Capital Area Food Bank (Southern Maryland)
description: A Feeding America member food bank serving southern Maryland, the District of Columbia, and northern Virginia. Distributes food through partner agencies and direct distribution programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in southern Maryland and the DC metro area

name: Maryland Department of Veterans Affairs
description: Serves Maryland veterans and their families with benefits counseling, claims assistance, employment support, and access to the Charlotte Hall Veterans Home. Maintains service offices across the state.
eligibilitySummary: Maryland veterans and their dependents

name: Maryland Crisis Hotline
description: Provides 24/7 crisis support and referrals for anyone experiencing mental health crisis, suicidal thoughts, or emotional distress. Connects callers with local crisis teams and resources.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Maryland experiencing a mental health or substance use crisis
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: Maryland Child Care Subsidy Program
description: Provides child care financial assistance to eligible families to help pay for child care while parents work or attend school. Covers licensed child care centers and registered family homes.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with children under 13 who are working or in approved activities
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 65% of state median income

name: Maryland TCA (Temporary Cash Assistance)
description: Provides temporary cash assistance and support services to low-income families with children. Includes employment preparation, job search assistance, and case management.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with dependent children under 18
incomeLimitNotes: Very low income limits; varies by family size

name: Maryland WIC Program
description: Provides nutritious foods, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and healthcare referrals for pregnant and postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant or postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 at nutritional risk
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of the federal poverty level

name: Maryland Children's Health Program (MCHP)
description: Maryland's Children's Health Insurance Program providing free or low-cost health coverage for uninsured children and pregnant women who are not eligible for traditional Medicaid.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children under 19 and pregnant women with income up to 317% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Children with family income up to 317% FPL

name: Maryland Transit Administration (MTA)
description: Operates bus, light rail, subway, and commuter rail in the Baltimore metropolitan area. Offers reduced fare programs for seniors, people with disabilities, and Medicare card holders.
eligibilitySummary: Baltimore area residents; reduced fares for seniors 65+, disabled, and Medicare holders
hours: Service varies by route; customer service Mon-Fri 6:00 AM - 7:00 PM

name: Baltimore City Department of Social Services
description: Provides social services to Baltimore residents including emergency assistance, food stamps, cash assistance, child welfare, and adult protective services.
eligibilitySummary: Baltimore City residents in need of social services

name: Montgomery County Health and Human Services
description: Provides comprehensive social and health services to Montgomery County residents including emergency assistance, housing support, behavioral health, and aging services.
eligibilitySummary: Montgomery County residents in need of health and social services
languages: English, Spanish, Chinese, Vietnamese, Korean, French

name: Prince George's County Social Services
description: Provides emergency assistance, housing support, food assistance, and other social services to residents of Prince George's County. Connects residents with federal and state benefit programs.
eligibilitySummary: Prince George's County residents in need of social services

name: Maryland Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free energy efficiency improvements to low-income homes including insulation, air sealing, and heating/cooling system upgrades to reduce energy costs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters at or below 200% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% of the federal poverty level

name: Maryland DORS Vocational Rehabilitation
description: The Division of Rehabilitation Services provides services to help people with disabilities prepare for, obtain, and maintain employment including career counseling, training, and job placement.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with physical or mental disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: Maryland Higher Education Commission - State Financial Aid
description: Administers state scholarships and grants for Maryland residents including the Guaranteed Access Grant, Educational Excellence Awards, and other need-based and merit-based financial aid programs.
eligibilitySummary: Maryland residents attending eligible postsecondary institutions
incomeLimitNotes: Guaranteed Access Grant for families with income up to 150% FPL; other programs vary

name: Maryland Adult Education
description: Provides free classes for adults including GED preparation, English language learning, basic literacy, and workforce readiness at locations across Maryland.
eligibilitySummary: Adults 18+ without a high school diploma or needing English language skills

name: 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline - Maryland
description: Provides free, confidential support for anyone in suicidal crisis or emotional distress. Call or text 988 to connect with a trained counselor 24/7.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing suicidal thoughts, mental health crisis, or emotional distress
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services in 150+ languages

name: Maryland Network Against Domestic Violence
description: Provides crisis support, safety planning, and referrals for survivors of domestic violence. Connects callers with local shelters, legal advocacy, and support services.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing domestic violence, dating violence, or sexual assault
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: Maryland Emergency Rental Assistance
description: Provides rental assistance to tenants behind on rent and at risk of eviction. Funds are paid directly to landlords on behalf of eligible tenants facing COVID-related or other financial hardships.
eligibilitySummary: Renters behind on rent and at risk of eviction due to financial hardship
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 80% of area median income

name: Maryland Disability Law Center
description: Provides free legal advocacy for people with disabilities in Maryland. Handles issues including abuse and neglect, discrimination, education, employment, housing, and access to services.
eligibilitySummary: People with disabilities in Maryland

name: Maryland RIDE Program - Senior Transportation
description: Provides transportation assistance for seniors and people with disabilities through local transit agencies and community transportation programs. Includes paratransit and demand-response services.
eligibilitySummary: Seniors 60+ and people with disabilities with limited transportation options
